rulings.Case Title: JERRY LANKFORD VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, CITY AND COUNTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 25, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff alleged eight causes of action. Plaintiff's first and second causes of action fail because he has not shown that he suffered an adverse employment action. Plaintiff's first cause of action also fails because he has not presented any admissible evidence that the City's legitimate, nondiscriminatory reason for placing him on administrative leave was a pretext for race discrimination. Plaintiff's second cause of action also fails because he has not presented any admissible evidence that the City's legitimate, nondiscriminatory reason for placing him on administrative leave was a pretext for retaliation against him for engaging in a protected activity. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit to his third, fifth and seventh causes of action. Plaintiff's fourth and eighth causes of action are barred due to plaintiff's failure to comply with the Government Tort Claims Act. Plaintiff's sixth cause of action was dismissed by the federal court. San Francisco Police Department has demonstrated legitimate, nondiscriminatory reasons for the administrative leave without pay. In turn, Mr. Lankford has not presented admissible evidence giving rise to an inference of unlawful discrimination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Furtehr Responses To Requests For Production; Requests For Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $1200 Against Pltf And Its Counsel, Nixon Peabody; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT COMPENSATION RISK MANAGERS OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, A, TWIN BRIDGES (BERMUDA), LTD., A FOREIGN, DANIEL HICKEY, CHESTER WALCZYK, MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD, COMPENSATION RISK MANAGERS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Furtehr Responses To Requests For Production; Requests For Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $1200 Against Pltf And Its Counsel, Nixon Peabody; Memo Of P&A; Declaration Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: THE NINTH AND MISSION CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A PUBLIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 25, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT ENCORE BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to maintain its summary judgment burden. Defendant's moving papers fail to demonstrate that the claim is time barred. Specifically, the moving papers do not show that the trustee repudiated the trust and triggered the running of the statute of limitations. The Court did not consider arguments made and evidence submitted for the first time in the reply papers or that was not included in defendant's separate statement of undisputed material facts. Judge Kahn discloses that more than fifteen years ago, and perhaps a lot longer than that, as an attorney he represented an entity or entities affiliated with the Barker Pacific Group in a lawsuit wholly unrelated to the subject of this case and that he believes that nothing about that prior representation causes him to be other than fair and impartial to all parties in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 25, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT MARK HOOSHMAND'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The face of the complaint and judicially noticeable documents do not clearly and necessarily show that plaintiffs' claims are time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CATON VS. BOEHRINGER INGELHEIM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 25, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT BOEHRINGER INGELHEIM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted as to the allegations that defendant failed to warn persons other than medical professionals and the allegations that misrepresentations were made to the FDA and denied as to the punitive damages allegations. Plaintiffs concede that the allegations that defendant failed to warn persons other than medical professionals and the allegations that misrepresentations were made to the FDA should be stricken. The punitive damages allegations are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CATON VS. BOEHRINGER INGELHEIM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 25, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT BOEHRINGER INGELHEIM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar as the third, fourth, seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action and overruled as to the fifth and sixth causes of action. The third, fourth, seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action were dismissed on September 12, 2012. The fifth and sixth causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
